final_preference_for_further_research: KTOS for next manual high-upside Serenity research; IRDM for validated defensive satellite infrastructure monitoring.

thesis_clarity.reason: IRDM is easier to map to satellite communications infrastructure because the network, EMSS, ECS3, SITH, and government revenue are directly disclosed. KTOS has a more complex mixed defense technology portfolio.

chokepoint_quality.reason: KTOS has stronger potential chokepoint angles in satellite ground systems, microwave electronics, TT&C/C2, mission systems, and defense electronics, but the exact segment materiality is not yet proven. IRDM is more infrastructure operator than hidden bottleneck.

commercial_validation.reason: IRDM has named government programs and values for EMSS, ECS3, and SITH. KTOS has strong backlog and satellite/microwave references, but less isolated satellite-specific contract evidence.

valuation_attractiveness.reason: IRDM has positive free cash flow and EV/EBITDA of 15.856. KTOS has negative free cash flow and much more stretched EV/EBITDA of 119.994, although KTOS has net cash.

cash_flow_quality.reason: IRDM has positive free cash flow of $253.0M. KTOS has negative free cash flow of $106.7M in company_profiles_v2.json.

upside_potential.reason: KTOS has a broader defense technology and satellite-ground optionality set that could re-rate if segment-specific backlog/contracts become visible. IRDM appears more mature and already repriced.

downside_risk.reason: IRDM has better cash flow but higher net debt and strong recent price momentum. KTOS has net cash but stretched multiples and negative free cash flow.

evidence_completeness.reason: IRDM has direct program-level contract values and service revenue detail. KTOS still needs more exact satellite-ground program revenue/backlog.

KTOS: Revenue specifically from satellite communications, ground systems, TT&C, C2, Space Domain Awareness, or microwave electronics for space/satellite applications.
Backlog dollars specifically tied to satellite/ground systems or defense space infrastructure.
Named customers/contracts and award values tied specifically to the space/defense satellite thesis.
Gross margin or operating margin specifically for the relevant space/satellite businesses.
Latest earnings call transcript and investor presentation were not retrieved; official IR pages timed out in this environment.
Ownership, short interest, analyst coverage, and peer/historical valuation context remain outside this package.
IRDM: Backlog evidence was not found in the retrieved filings; the 10-Q says contracts generally do not contain performance obligations beyond one year, so undisclosed backlog should not be inferred.
Segment revenue and segment margin for EMSS, ECS3, SITH, hosted payload, PNT, Certus, and commercial satellite services are not fully separated in the available evidence.
Current EMSS renewal economics beyond September 2026 are not proven in the cached evidence.
Satellite replacement-cycle capex timing and long-term maintenance economics need more direct support.
Latest earnings call transcript and investor presentation were not retrieved in this package.
Ownership, short interest, analyst coverage, peer valuation history, and customer/program-level concentration beyond disclosed U.S. government exposure remain missing.

specific_answers.is_irdm_better_or_safer: IRDM is safer and more commercially validated, but not necessarily the better Serenity candidate. It is more of a stable satellite communications infrastructure operator than a high-upside hidden chokepoint.
specific_answers.does_ktos_have_more_upside_weaker_eviden: Yes. KTOS has more upside optionality if satellite-ground, microwave electronics, and defense-space program evidence becomes more specific, but current evidence is weaker and valuation/cash-flow support is worse.
specific_answers.does_irdm_have_stronger_evidence_lower_r: Yes. IRDM has stronger hard evidence and cash flow, but lower re-rating potential because its government satellite communications role is visible and recent price momentum is already strong.
specific_answers.next_manual_research_priority: KTOS should be the next manual research priority for the Serenity framework because the unresolved question is potentially value-creating: whether satellite-ground and defense-space exposure is large enough to justify a High Priority thesis. IRDM should remain monitored for EMSS renewal, FCF durability, debt/capex, and valuation reset.
specific_answers.should_either_upgrade_high_priority: No. KTOS lacks segment-specific hard evidence and has valuation/free-cash-flow issues. IRDM lacks a clear underappreciated re-rating mechanism and has missing backlog/segment economics and debt/capex questions.
